# Copilot Instructions

## Project overview

`yamlutil` is a small Go CLI (Cobra + Viper) for working with YAML files. Entry
point is `main.go`, which delegates to `cmd.Execute()`. Subcommands live as
separate files in `cmd/`:

- `cmd/root.go` â€” root command, Viper config wiring (looks for `$HOME/.yamlutil.yaml`)
- `cmd/list.go` â€” `list` subcommand, recursively prints all dotted/array-indexed keys
- `cmd/sort.go` â€” `sort` subcommand, sorts top-level keys alphabetically

Cross-cutting helper:

- `argsprocessor/args.go` â€” `ProcessArgs` iterates over filenames in `args`, or
  falls back to a single stdin read when `args` is empty. The constant
  `argsprocessor.STDIN` (value `"-"`) is the sentinel used to denote stdin
  across the codebase â€” match this convention when adding input handling.

## Build, test, lint

There is **no Makefile, no linter config, and no test suite in this repo.** Use
the standard Go toolchain:

- Build: `go build -o yamlutil .` (run from the repo root)
- Run: `go run . <subcommand> [args...]` (e.g. `go run . list sample.yaml`)
- Format: `gofmt -w .` and `goimports -w .` (matches the import grouping style
  in `cmd/*.go`: stdlib, then third-party, separated by blank lines)
- Vet: `go vet ./...`
- Tests: there are none today. If you add a test file, run a single test with
  `go test ./pkg -run TestName` and the full suite with `go test ./...`.
  Module path is `github.com/sidecut/yamlutil` (see `go.mod`); Go 1.18.

## Conventions and gotchas

- **Cobra pattern**: every subcommand file in `cmd/` declares a package-level
  `*cobra.Command` variable (`listCmd`, `sortCmd`) and registers it with
  `rootCmd.AddCommand(...)` from an `init()`. Follow this pattern for any new
  subcommand â€” do not call `rootCmd.AddCommand` from `main.go`.
- **Flag validation lives in `Run`**, not in a separate `PreRun`/`Args`
  validator. See `sortCmd.Run` for the style: build an `errors.New(...)` and
  pass to `cobra.CheckErr(err)`. Use `cobra.CheckErr` for any error returned
  from command logic.
- **YAML library is `gopkg.in/yaml.v2`** (not v3). Map types are
  `map[string]interface{}` for `sort` and `map[interface{}]interface{}` (aliased
  as `genericMap`) for `list` because `list` must preserve non-string keys
  (`sample.yaml` has keys like `0`, `false`, `null`). When adding features that
  need both behaviors, mirror this split rather than unifying it.
- **Reading input**: always go through `argsprocessor.ProcessArgs` for the
  file-or-stdin branching. The callback receives a `filename` and an
  `io.Reader`; output filename prefixes (printed when more than one file is
  supplied) are the caller's responsibility â€” see the `len(args) > 1 &&
  filename != argsprocessor.STDIN` check repeated in both `list.go` and
  `sort.go`.
- **Key formatting in `list`**: `fullKey` joins parent and child with `.` and
  quotes non-string children. Array elements are addressed as `key[i]`. Keep
  this format stable â€” `sort` does not currently produce these paths, so any
  future cross-command tooling will depend on this exact syntax.
- **Sort output**: `writeSortedMap` calls `yaml.Marshal` on the parsed map and
  writes the raw bytes. Because `yaml.v2` re-emits a `map[string]interface{}`,
  the sort is effectively only at the **top level**. If a recursive sort is
  added, it must be done in-memory before re-marshaling â€” `yaml.Marshal` alone
  will not sort nested maps.
- **Sample fixtures** (`sample.yaml`, `sample2.yaml`) intentionally contain
  weird keys (`0`, `false`, `null`, YAML booleans like `Yes`, comments). They
  are the regression suite for this project â€” use them to verify behavior
  rather than discarding them.
- **License header**: every `.go` file starts with the Apache 2.0 boilerplate
  generated by `cobra-cli init` (with placeholder `NAME HERE <EMAIL ADDRESS>`).
  Preserve it in new files; this repo has not customized it.
- **No external test/lint/CI config is checked in.** Don't assume a CI workflow
  exists on GitHub â€” verify with `gh workflow list` before adding CI-specific
  instructions to PRs.
